_note: hoop/v094 — the Chapter One OPENING geometry: a hand-authored prologue region (Bay 14 → the Nave → the industrial margin → the rind shaft), the bible's named scenes. Features PIN a specific content_item via `content_id` (the authored-placement path added to engine.js#interact) so Olo is at the cradle and Sevin at the margin — not a procedural roll. THE KEYSTONE still holds: when this engine drops onto the infinite ship, each `key` becomes a chamber address (js/postal.js); the named opening and the endless Nave crowd (procedural, no content_id) share one engine. The terminal carries `fixture:true` — the client renders it as a wall fixture you walk adjacent to and interact with (the mythograph reader). The shaft membrane is locked (locks.js) on flag.sevin_believes — the road into chunk 2. Glyphs: #=wall .=floor +=door.

zones.bay14: Reconstruction Bay 14 — where you wake. Off every schematic. Walls stencilled in a notation no one in the Nave uses.
zones.nave: The Nave — street-level city, three factions sharing space. The reading-room and its Tabard terminal sit here.
zones.margin: The Industrial Margin — where the cozy city gives way to machinery and the air tastes different. Sevin works here.
zones.shaft: The Rind Access Shaft — sealed officially, open unofficially to whoever Sevin believes.

features.label: a wall console — the access log
a stencil worked into the wall
a Tabard terminal, a mythograph glowing on it
the rind access shaft, faintly warm
features._lock: locks.js seals the membrane into the shaft zone until flag.sevin_believes — the deterministic lock layer the live map already uses. Sevin's dialogue sets the fact.
